saints.name: Afterfeast of the Theophany of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

troparion.text: When You, O Lord were baptized in the Jordan / the worship of the Trinity was made manifest / for the voice of the Father bore witness to You / and called You His beloved Son. / And the Spirit, in the form of a dove, / confirmed the truthfulness of His word. / O Christ, our God, You have revealed Yourself / and have enlightened the world, glory to You!

kontakion.text: Today You have shown forth to the world, O Lord, / and the light of Your countenance has been marked on us. / Knowing You, we sing Your praises. / You have come and revealed Yourself, / O unapproachable Light.

troparion.text: In truth you were revealed to your flock as a rule of faith, / an image of humility and a teacher of abstinence; / your humility exalted you; / your poverty enriched you. / Hierarch Father Gregory, / entreat Christ our God / that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: You kept watch with the eyes of your soul, holy bishop, / revealing yourself as a watchful pastor for the world. / With the staff of your wisdom and your fervent intercession, / you drove away all heretics like wolves. / You preserved your flock free from harm, most wise Gregory!

troparion.text: O God of our Fathers, / always act with kindness towards us; / take not Your mercy from us, / but guide our lives in peace / through the prayers of the hierarchs Gregory and Dometian.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
O Hierarch Dometian, divine thunder, spiritual trumpet, /   planter of faith and pruner of heresies, great favorite of the Trinity, /   while standing with the Angels before God,  /  pray unceasingly for us all.

saints.name: Saint Marcian the Presbyter in Constantinople

troparion.text:
In you the image was preserved with exactness, O Father,  / for taking up your cross, you followed
after Christ.  / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away,  / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal.  / Therefore, most venerable Father Marcian your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “Seeking the Highest...”)
Having divinely armed thyself with the purity of thy soul  / and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a
spear, /  thou, our Father Marcian, have slain the armies of the demons. /  Therefore, pray unceasingly for us all.

saints.name: Venerable Paul, Abbot of Obnora, Vologda

troparion.text: Inflamed with divine love from your youth, O venerable Paul, and despising all earthly attractions, you came to love Christ alone,  / and for His sake, you settled in the inner wilderness /  and lived with the wild animals, entirely for Christ.  / Thus, the All-Seeing Eye, having beheld your labors, /  enriched you with the gift of miracles, even after your repose.  / Therefore, we cry out to you:  / Pray unceasingly for us who always revere your honorable memory with hymns of praise.

kontakion.text: Turning all of your spiritual longing toward God, steadfastly you followed His call with all your soul.  / Having settled in the wilderness and, having lived there in an angelic way, you were a path to salvation for many.  / Hence, Christ has glorified you, and enriched you with the gift of miracles. /  Therefore, we cry out to you: Hail, Venerable Paul, dweller in the wilderness.

saints.name: Venerable Makarios of Pisma and Kostroma

troparion.text: In you the image was preserved with exactness, O Father,  / for taking up your cross, you followed after Christ.  / By so doing, you taught us to disregard the flesh, for it passes away,  / but to care instead for the soul, since it is immortal.  / Therefore, most venerable Father Makarios your spirit rejoices with the Angels.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Having divinely armed yourself with the purity of your soul,  / and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a spear, you pierced the armies of demons.  / O our Father Makarios, pray unceasingly for us all.

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Antipas, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

saints.name: Saint Theophan the Recluse, Bishop of Tambov

troparion.text: O guide of Orthodoxy, teacher of piety and purity, / ascetic of Vysha, divinely wise and holy hierarch Theophan:  / By your writings you explained the Word of God,  /  and showed the way of salvation unto all the faithful. / Entreat Christ God that our souls may be saved.

kontakion.text: Holy hierarch Theophan, namesake of Theophany, /  you enlightened many people by your teachings. /  Now that you stand before the throne of the Holy Trinity  /  together with the angels,  /  pray without ceasing for us all.
